rulings.Rulings: The question before the court at the hearing on 2/23/18 was whether under German law, Ursula Lieder had been adopted by Elsa Leider. To assist the court in making this determination, both parties hired German lawyers experienced in German Inheritance Law. After reviewing all the adoption agreements and documents prepared in 1952, Petitioner's expert, Matthies Braun, opined that Ursula had been adopted by the married couple, which included Elsa. Respondent's expert, Viet Kinger, contended that under German law the adoption contract controlled, and pursuant to that document, Ursula had been adopted only by Otto and not Elsa. Petitioners have now produced a declaration by a new a different expert, who contends that a change in German law in 1966, after Ursula had left Germany and was living in California, transformed the adoption into a joint adoption without the knowledge or consent of any of the adopting parties. This is simply too little too late. Both parties had ample opportunity to present evidence of all aspects of relevant German law before the hearing on 2/23/18, and neither of the parties' experts made any reference to the 1966 statute. The Motion for Reconsideration is denied. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) and opposing parties no later than 4:00 p.m. Wednesday, April 11, 2018.
